questionText: API latency is variable & app is sluggish. How do you design UI safely?

whatWorkedWell: Strong understanding of optimistic UI patterns — correctly identified when to update the UI before server confirmation to reduce perceived latency.
Good use of skeleton screens and shimmer placeholders to maintain visual continuity during data fetches.
Mentioned debouncing and throttling API calls to prevent UI jank from rapid state updates.
Demonstrated awareness of caching strategies using stale-while-revalidate to serve content instantly.
overallTakeaways: Consider discussing error boundaries and fallback UI more explicitly — what happens when the optimistic update fails?
Could strengthen the answer by mentioning specific tools like React Query or SWR for data fetching patterns.
The explanation of background sync was solid but could include retry strategies with exponential backoff.
Overall a well-structured response that covered both user experience and technical implementation.

keyMoments.description: Opened strong by framing the problem as a UX challenge, not just a technical one.
Missed opportunity to mention connection-aware loading — adapting UI based on network speed.
Excellent explanation of optimistic updates with rollback on failure scenarios.
Could have elaborated more on how to prioritize which API calls are critical vs deferrable.
Strong closing — tied everything back to measurable metrics like Time to Interactive and First Contentful Paint.
